title: Replace wave mode with 3D secondary mode
overview: Add a fourth 3D mode ('crystal') to the mode rotation. The crystal mode renders a volumetric point cloud using faceted/crystalline deformation via a custom shader. No WaveField.ts file exists to delete — the ticket's intent is to ensure all modes are volumetric 3D, which the existing three already are; this adds a fourth sculptural variant. The mode manager, debug overlay, and audio pipeline are preserved.

steps.title: Add crystal volumetric shapes to volumetricPoints.ts
steps.description: Add two new VolumetricShape variants ('crystalCluster' and 'geode') to the generator. crystalCluster distributes points in clustered icosahedral groupings with angular offsets. geode creates a hollow faceted shell with interior scatter. Both use the existing PRNG-seeded pattern.
details: Add 'crystalCluster' | 'geode' to the VolumetricShape union type
Add both to the VOLUMETRIC_SHAPES array
Implement fillCrystalCluster: 5-8 cluster centers (seeded), each with points distributed in a tight angular arrangement around the center, radius 0.3-0.8 per cluster, total spread ~2.5
Implement fillGeode: outer faceted shell (radius 2.0, thickness 0.15) with ~30% of points scattered inside at smaller radii (0.3-1.5), giving a hollow-crystal-interior feel
Follow existing patterns: rng-based, write directly to Float32Array output

steps.title: Create crystal shader pair
steps.description: Create crystalWarp.vert.glsl and crystalWarp.frag.glsl. The vertex shader reuses the same uniform/attribute interface as pointWarp but applies faceted deformation: quantized noise displacement that snaps to angular increments, giving a crystalline/geometric feel distinct from the organic pointCloud mode.
details: Vertex shader: prepend noise3d.glsl (same pattern as pointWarp.vert.glsl)

Same 18 uniforms as pointWarp: uTime, uBassEnergy, uTrebleEnergy, uOpacity, uMotionAmplitude, uPointerDisturbance, uPointerPos, uPaletteHue, uPaletteSaturation, uCadence, uBreathScale, uBasePointSize, uNoiseFrequency, uRadialScale, uTwistStrength, uFieldSpread, uNoiseOctaves, uEnablePointerRepulsion, uEnableSlowModulation, uDisplacementScale
Same 4 attributes: position(3), size(1), aHueOffset(1), aRandom(3)
Key difference: displacement is quantized — fbm3 output is floored to discrete steps (e.g. floor(noise * 4.0) / 4.0) before applying, creating faceted/angular movement
Bass drives macro displacement scale (multiply displacement by 1.0 + uBassEnergy * 0.6)
Treble drives point size variation: gl_PointSize multiplied by (1.0 + uTrebleEnergy * 0.4) for sparkle effect
Rotation uses two-axis (Y + X tilt) for more sculptural tumbling, controlled by uCadence
Fragment shader: similar to pointWarp.frag.glsl but with sharper falloff (step edge blend instead of smooth circle) for faceted point appearance, still respects uOpacity and vertex color

steps.title: Create crystalField.ts geometry system
steps.description: Implement the crystal mode as a GeometrySystem following the exact same pattern as pointCloud.ts. Uses the crystal shapes and crystal shaders.
details: Export CrystalFieldConfig interface matching PointCloudConfig shape: maxPoints, enableSparkle, noiseOctaves, enablePointerRepulsion, enableSlowModulation
Export createCrystalField(config?) returning GeometrySystem

init(): use createPRNG(seed + ':crystal'), select shape from ['crystalCluster', 'geode'] only, compute effectiveCount from density * maxPoints (cap at maxPoints), generate positions via generateVolumetricPoints, create geometry with same 5 attributes (position, color, size, aHueOffset, aRandom), create ShaderMaterial with crystal shaders and same 18 uniforms
draw(): update all uniforms from frame.params (same as pointCloud.draw), apply two-axis rotation (Y from drift + bass, X from cadence-driven tilt), apply z-breathing
setOpacity(): set uOpacity uniform
cleanup(): remove mesh, dispose geometry + material, null refs
Use 0.8x maxPoints scaling: effectiveCount capped at Math.round(maxPoints * 0.8) to keep crystal mode slightly less dense for visual clarity

steps.title: Verify no 2D/flat geometries remain
steps.description: Audit all mode files to confirm every geometry system uses 3D volumetric points with perspective depth. No 2D line geometries, flat plane geometries, or Canvas2D rendering should exist.
details: Confirm all four systems use THREE.Points + BufferGeometry with 3-component position attributes
Confirm all use ShaderMaterial with 3D displacement in vertex shaders
Confirm no THREE.Line, THREE.LineSegments, THREE.PlaneGeometry, or Canvas2D usage exists
No code changes expected — this is a verification step

steps.title: Register crystal mode in main.ts
steps.description: Wire the crystal mode into the mode manager and update the debug modes array so the debug overlay correctly reports mode name and point count when crystal is active.
details: Import createCrystalField from './visual/systems/crystalField'

After pointCloud creation (~line 111), add: const crystal = createCrystalField({ maxPoints: Math.round(quality.maxPoints * 0.8), enableSparkle: quality.enableSparkle, noiseOctaves: quality.noiseOctaves, enablePointerRepulsion: quality.enablePointerRepulsion, enableSlowModulation: quality.enableSlowModulation })
Add crystal to modeManager array: { name: 'crystal', factory: () => crystal } as 4th entry
Add crystal to debug modes array (~line 130): { name: 'crystal', maxPoints: Math.round(quality.maxPoints * 0.8) } so getModeName and getPointCount work correctly for the crystal mode

steps.title: Build verification and console check
steps.description: Run npm run build and verify no errors. Start dev server and check browser console for shader compile errors, WebGL validation errors, or runtime exceptions.
details: npm run build must succeed with no TypeScript errors
No console errors on load (shader compile, WebGL validate, runtime)
Crystal mode must render visible 3D points with depth/parallax
Mode transitions must work smoothly between all four modes
Audio pipeline must remain connected — bass/treble reactivity visible in crystal mode
Mute toggle and info overlay must still function

testing.description: Crystal mode renders 3D volumetric points with perspective depth
testing.verify: When crystal mode is active, scene shows a volumetric point cloud with visible parallax on camera drift. Points are not flat/coplanar.
No WebGL shader compile errors, VALIDATE_STATUS errors, or 'invalid program' errors in console when crystal mode initializes.
Points visibly displace in 3D space over time with faceted/angular character distinct from the smooth organic pointCloud mode.

testing.description: No 2D line or flat plane geometries remain
testing.verify: Grep codebase for THREE.Line, THREE.LineSegments, THREE.PlaneGeometry, Canvas2D — none found in visual systems.
Transition from any mode to crystal and back: audio continues playing, bass drives macro motion in crystal mode, treble drives point sparkle. No audio graph teardown on switch.
Crossfade between modes shows both rendering simultaneously with opacity blending over 2-4 seconds. No flash or gap.

testing.description: Debug overlay shows correct crystal mode info
testing.verify: When crystal mode is active, debug overlay shows 'crystal' as mode name and correct point count (0.8x maxPoints tier value).
npm run build completes successfully with zero TypeScript or bundler errors.

architectural_notes: Crystal uses the same uniform interface as all other modes — no new quality.ts fields needed
0.8x point count scaling keeps crystal visually distinct (sparser, more defined structure) while staying within the quality tier budget
Two new volumetric shapes (crystalCluster, geode) are added to the shared generator — they are also available to pointCloud mode via random selection, which enriches that mode as well
Crystal's two-axis rotation (Y + X tilt) creates a more sculptural tumbling motion compared to the single-axis Y rotation used by pointCloud and ribbon
The faceted shader (quantized noise displacement + step-edge fragment) gives crystal a visually distinct character without requiring new uniforms or attributes
